plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: SMILES is O=N, representing NO. Atom list shows O at index 0 (SP2, degree 1, no radical) and N at index 1 (SP2, degree 1, no radical). No hybridization mismatch with user request. No radicals present, so adsorbate_type is 'Molecule'. History is None, so this is the first attempt. Analyzing adsorbate: NO has major functional group as a diatomic molecule with lone pairs on N and O, typically adsorbs via N atom. Surface analysis: Available sites include ontop, bridge, and hollow types with compositions involving Ti, In, N. For lowest energy configuration, start with a common mode for NO: adsorption via N at a bridge site on metal atoms. Ti is a transition metal likely to provide strong binding. Select bridge Ti-Ti site, binding via N (index 1). This is physically consistent. Since hard limit is 1 attempt and termination is disabled, but this is the first, action is 'continue'.
